title: The UK Today
overview: Modern Britain is a diverse and democratic nation of around 67 million people. Its four countries—England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland—share one government and monarch while each keeps its own identity and culture. The UK has a strong economy, free education, health care through the NHS, and a deep commitment to human rights and equality.

study_sections.heading: Basic Facts
study_sections.content: Population: about 67 million. Four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland. Capital cities: London, Edinburgh, Cardiff, Belfast. Official language: English (plus Welsh, Gaelic, Irish). Government: constitutional monarchy and parliamentary democracy. Currency: pound sterling (£).

study_sections.heading: Economy and Services
study_sections.content: Main industries: finance, technology, manufacturing, education, creative arts. NHS (1948) provides free health care. Education compulsory 5 to 18 years old. Service sector dominates economy.

study_sections.heading: Rights and Values
study_sections.content: Equality Act 2010 and Human Rights Act 1998 protect freedom and fairness. Diverse, multi-ethnic population. Promotes respect for all communities.

study_sections.heading: Global Role
study_sections.content: Member of Commonwealth of Nations and United Nations. Britain leads in science, medicine, film, music, and design. Public services include police, fire service, armed forces. BBC provides public broadcasting. Pinewood Studios produces films.

key_facts: Four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, NI
Capital cities: London, Edinburgh, Cardiff, Belfast
NHS stands for: National Health Service
memory_hook: UK Today as smartphone specs: Hardware (67M people in 4 countries), OS (constitutional monarchy + democracy), Apps (NHS healthcare, education 5-18, BBC, Pinewood), Security (Human Rights Act 1998, Equality Act 2010), Currency (£), Network (UN, Commonwealth), Core Values (respect, diversity, fairness), Storage (London capital + Edinburgh/Cardiff/Belfast), Language (English + Welsh/Gaelic/Irish). Fully updated modern system!

options: England, Isle of Man, Scotland, Wales
